name: Sydney (New South Wales, Australia)
description: A blue field bearing a white saltire (St Andrew's Cross), with the coat of arms of the City of Sydney centred on the saltire's intersection.
history: The flag of the City of Sydney displays the city's coat of arms (granted in 1908) on a white saltire on a blue field, in the manner of a defaced Scottish saltire. The saltire references Sydney's namesake, Lord Sydney (Thomas Townshend), and the Scottish heritage among the colony's early administrators.

features.role: Coat of arms of the City of Sydney
